publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-07-10 18:20:44 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Printed materials, newspaper clippings, posters, and ephemera.
did.abstract: Political publications, brochures, posters, banners, handbills and other ephemera relating to national and local elections.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Political publications, brochures, posters, banners, handbills and other ephemera relating to national and local elections of 8 May 1995 and 14 May 2001. Official publications relating to various government programs; family planning, National Reconciliation and Development Program, land reform, U.S.A.-Philippine relations, and tourism. Transcripts of speeches given by President Ferdinand E. Marcos and Imelda Romualdez Marcos at the 19th General Conference of the International Union for the Scientific Study of Population, Manila, 1981. Presidential Decree Nos. 316 (1973), 815 (1975) and 816.

In addition the collection includes educational and promotional materials relating to the 2004 election in the Philippines. The first part consist of materials that provide a better understanding of the 2004 Philippine Synchronized Election. These materials were produced by organizations such as NAMFREL and COMELEC and included both instructions concerning the election process as well as lists of candidates. Samples of pre-election surveys and a bundle of newspaper clippings from the "Philippine Daily Inquirer" concerning the election are also included.

There are also promotional materials for the presidential and senatorial and local candidates such as smear pamphlets, comics, stickers, posters, cards, calendars, banners and sample ballots. The candidates represented are Fernando Poe Jr. (FJP) and his vice presidential candidate Loren Legarda, Gloria Macapagal Arroyo and her vice president candidate Noli de Castro, Raul Roco and his vice president partner Hermie Aquino, presidential candidate Ping Lacson and presidential candidate Bro Eddie. Senatorial candidates included are Manuel Roxas, Salvador Escudero, Rodolfo Biazon, Alfred Lim, Boots Anson-Roa, Carlos Padilla, Juan Ponce Enrile, Ernesto Maceda, Dr. Parouk Hussin, Ernesto Herrera, Jinggoy Estrada, Robert Barbers, Roman Revilla, Richard Gordon, Aquilino Pimentel, Pia Cayeteno, Jamby Madrigal, Miriam Defensor Santiago, Francisco Cahvez, Didagen Dilangalen, Francisco Tatad, Robert Jaworski and John Henry Osmeña. Parties represented in this collection are Forward Taguig, the Performance Team, Batang Makati Movement, Lakas-CMD, Dodot Henry Lito (DHL), Lakas ng Batangas. Geographical areas included are Makati, Taguig, Quezon, Navotas, Pasay

odd.head: General
odd.p: An item level list of contents of box 1 can be found in the accession folder.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Philippines Miscellany, #6505. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Mindanao Coalition of Development NGO Networks

scopecontent.p: Pamphlet, Commission on Elections, Voter Information Sheet, List of Candidates and sample ballot

did.unittitle: COMELEC List of Registered/Accredited Political Parties
Bakit si FPJ ang dapat iboto?
COMELEC General instructions for election inspectors

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet, General instructions for the board of election inspectors on the casting and counting of votes

scopecontent.p: March and April edition, publishing dates 5 and 23 April 2004

scopecontent.p: Pamphlet, General instructions for municipal/city/provincial and district boards

scopecontent.p: Paper by Julio C. Teehankee Ph.D. De La Salle University

did.unittitle: Pulse Asia's national survey on 2004 elections

Priorities Philippines "A turning point in the first 360 days"
Promotional material FJP and Loren Legarda
did.physdesc: 3 pamphlets, 6 sample ballots, 1 card, 1 calendar, 3 stickers, 2 booklets

did.unittitle: Promotional material Gloria Arrayo and Noli de Castro
did.physdesc: 6 sample ballots, 1 booklet, 1 calendar, 2 stickers, 1 pamphlet

did.unittitle: Promotional material Raul Roco and Hermie Aquino
Philippine Daily Inquirer, issues Vol. 19 no. 115 - 152
COMELEC Information kit for the May 10, 2004 synchronized national and local elections
Kim Atienza and Lito Atienza, Cita Astals
Performance Team: Baby Ramos Pangilinan, Divine Jacome, Pasia, Tolentino Jr. and Isreal Cruzado
Vote for Councilor: Peralta, Astals, CalinginDaba, Advincula, Benaldo, Guia, Cabanlas, Jongko
Vote for Mayor/ Vice- Mayor: Tamondong, Belmonte / Alcera and Bagatsing
1st District Navotas: Ramirez, Cruz, Peña, Plamenco
2nd District Navotas: Santos, Gino-Gino, Patis, Boyet Mendez Chu, Lizan
2nd District Taguig: Reyes, Tinga, Bartolome and Minglana
2nd District Makati: Paraan, Torrente, Tercero, Maccay, Lapasaran, Yabut, Ibay,
2nd Distric Parañaque: Manny de Guia and Lakas-CMD Bert Bonsol
5th District Manila: Hizon, Cuna and Ocampo
Toby Tiangco for Mayor, FPJ for President
Riolo Golez: Support Gloria for President
Loren Legarda - Vice President Candidate Poster
Gloria Macapagal Arroyo for President Posters
FPJ For President and Loren Learda as Vice President
Partido Navoteño: Santos, Tiangco, Cruz, Boyet Mendez Chu, Ramirez, Lagman, Plamenco, Gino-Gino, Santiago
Parañaque City: Rico Golez, Beng Amurao
Vote for Councilor: Sotto, Minglana, King Yabut, Marquez, Dguia
Misc: Chipeco for Congressman and Bistex for Vice-Mayor
